rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SCOTT KIN WAH CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of James Chan?S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Served By Dong Chen
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Objector James Chan's Motion to Quash Subpoenas Served by Dong Chen on the Decedent's attorney Catherine Yee, Esq. is DENIED. Under the Discovery Act, service of a deposition subpoena on a nonparty must be by personal service pursuant to CCP Section 2020.220. The record indicates that Chen attempted to personally serve attorney Yee at her office during business hours, but access was not granted to attorney Yee's suite by the building security guard or Yee's receptionist. Thereafter, personal service was attempted at attorney Yee's home, where the subpoenas were left with a man over the age of 18. Chen further mailed a copy of the subpoenas to both attorney Yee and Chan and emailed a copy to attorney Yee. In several meet-and-confer efforts Chen attempted to informally resolve this issue, including offering to re-serve the subpoenas in compliance with CCP Section 2020.220. Chan does not dispute that neither he nor attorney Yee responded to the offer to re-serve the subpoenas, nor does Chan dispute that both he and attorney Yee received the subpoenas. The deficiency in the service is excusable, and, in any event, Chan has not demonstrated prejudice and no substantial prejudice is apparent on this record. The purpose of a notice to consumer is to give the "consumer" a chance to seek an order protecting the privacy of his records. CCP Section 2020.510(c), 1985.3(e). In this case, the Decedent was the consumer. It is well settled that the right of privacy is purely a personal one; it cannot be asserted by anyone other than the person whose privacy has been invaded. Lugosi v. Universal Pictures (1979) 25 Cal.App.3d 813, 820-821. The right does not survive but dies with the person. Hendrickson v. California Newspapers (1975) 48 Cal.App.3d 59, 62. The Decedent's right of privacy died with him, and no notice to consumer was required prior to serving the subpoenas. Courts liberally construe the scope of discovery, and discovery is allowed so long as the information sought to be discovered is either itself admissible or "reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." CCP Section 2017.010. The burden is on Chan to show that the information sought is irrelevant, and Chan has failed to make that showing. The attorney is the holder of the attorney work product privilege. The initial burden to assert this privilege is on the attorney, and attorney Yee has not raised any objections to the subpoenas or asserted any privilege claims. The holder of the attorney-client privilege is the client (here the Decedent). The attorney-client privilege terminates when there is no personal representative to claim it. HLC Properties, Ltd. V. Sup. Ct. 35 Cal.4th 54, 65-66. In this case, a personal representative has not been appointed so no holder of the privilege exists to assert it. Evidence Code Section 954. For the reasons stated, the motion to quash the subpoenas is DENIED.

rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Chen's request for sanctions is DENIED. While Chen is the prevailing party on this motion the Court does not find that the motion was made in bad faith or without substantial justification. Petitioner Dong Chen shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
